Improvement Applied: Benchmark forecast using same-week historical behavior.
Key Parameters: No complex tuning; simple seasonal benchmark.
Why It Matters: Establishes the minimum reference point for evaluating improvement.
Improved because restaurant demand has temporal and weekly seasonal patterns.

Improvement Applied: Added Random Forest residual correction to SARIMAX.
Feature Strategy: SARIMAX temporal structure + nonlinear lag-based residual learning.
Why It Matters: Best result because it combines time-series structure with nonlinear residual correction.

Improvement Applied: Predicted net sales from predicted transactions and predicted average ticket.
Why It Matters: More explainable for managers because it decomposes sales into operational drivers.

Improvement Applied: Implemented validation-based conformal prediction intervals around the Hybrid SARIMAX + Random Forest forecast.
Feature Strategy: Uses rolling backtesting absolute residuals to estimate uncertainty around the point forecast.
Why It Matters: Adds uncertainty-aware planning through prediction intervals. It is evaluated by coverage, conformal margin, and interval width, not by MAE/RMSE/MAPE as a separate point-forecast model.
